calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called together:
1) PES-19-303207, Estate of Linda Yee Lin Ma; and
2) PES-19-303208, Estate of Matthew Shiu Sang Ma.

Appearances:
Amy L. Hespenheide for executor Melissa Ka Yee Ma.
Dwight Dee, counsel for executor in Canadian case (via Zoom).

The Court called the matters regarding the amended third account and petition for final distribution.

The Court noted that the probate examiner raised concerns about protection of minor beneficiaries. Ms. Hespenheide explained that under the wills of the decedents, Melissa Ma was appointed executor and trustee of the testamentary trusts to be established in Canada, with authority to appoint a corporate co-trustee. She further clarified that Melissa herself is also a beneficiary of the trusts, in addition to her descendants.

Ms. Hespenheide provided context regarding the Canadian proceedings, stating that those matters are pending resolution of the California ancillary cases before distributions can be completed, including distributions to charitable beneficiaries and funding of the testamentary trusts.

Following discussion, the Court waived any irregularities and took the petitions under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Continued To Oct-28-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Amy Lynn Hespenheide (attorney) to appear on October 28, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
Off Calendar Mar-19-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-19-303207 Estate of Linda Yee Lin Ma and
PES-19-303208 Estate of Matthew Shui Sang Ma.

Appearances: Amy Hespenheide appeared by zoom for Melissa Ma (executor).

The court accepts Mrs. Hespenheide's request to accept the report and the interim account as an amendment so that she can then file for final distribution. The court informed Mrs. Hespenheide that the proposed orders need to be revised. The Petitions for Approval of the Second Interim Accounts and Reports are under submission.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
Off Calendar Feb-26-2024 Continued To Mar-18-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
Off Calendar Jan-22-2024 Continued To Feb-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-19-303207 the Estate of Linda Yee Lin Ma and
PES-19-303208 the Estate of Matthew Shui Sang Ma.

Appearances: Amy Hespenheide appeared by zoom for Melissa Ma (executor).

The court stated that it doesn't understand why it has taken so long for counsel to address the issues that were raised in the probate examiner's notes nine months ago and noted that this her fourth appearance without filing anything. Mrs. Hespenheide stated that she filed petitions to increase the bonds in both matters last week, that she was dealing with a family illness in the month of December, that the hang up is getting the statements from Wells Fargo for the appropriate time period and asked for permission to file bank statements for the estate account which now includes the unclaimed funds, instead of the statements for the joint bank account. Mrs. Hespenheide also stated that she filed declarations today. Discussion was held with regard to the court providing something to Wells Fargo if needed and the issue of whether getting waivers from the charitable beneficiaries in Canada has been considered. Mrs. Hespenheide stated that she thought that getting waivers was not going to be an option, but that she will speak with her counterpart in Canada, and that she does not have the 2022 tax returns yet. The Petitions for Approval of Second Interim Account and Report of Executor are continued to February 26, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
Off Calendar Nov-06-2023 Continued To Jan-22-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-19-303207 The Estate of Linda Yee Lin Ma and PES-19-303208 The Estate of Matthew Shui Sang Ma.

Appearances: Amy Hespenheide appeared via zoom for Melissa Kay Yee Ma (petitioner).

Ms. Hespenheide request a one-month continuance to address all the issues in the Courts tentative ruling, and that she should have the second accounting petition filed before the next hearing date. Petition for approval of second interim account and report of executor's are both continued to January 22, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Prieto: Not reported.

Ms. Hespenheide stated that she is still working on the amended petition, and that she is working on the security deposits from Berry Street property. Both matters are continued to November 06, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Napata: Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Continued To Oct-09-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-19-303207 Estate of Linda Yee Lin Ma and
PES-19-303208 Estate of Matthew Shui Sang Ma.

Appearances: Amy Hespenheide appeared by zoom for Melissa Ma (executor).

Mrs. Hespenheide apologized for not responding to the probate examiner's notes stating that she was on medical leave from April 24th through May 30th, that her colleague was unable to follow up on this and that she picked it back up in June. Mrs. Hespenheide also stated that she is in the process of collecting the statements, that she needs to file an ex parte petition to increase the bond once amounts are determined, that there are no assignments or powers of appointment, and that there is no way to know the order of death of Mr. and Mrs. Ma because they died in an automobile accident. In addition, Mrs. Hespenheide stated that the executor is not only an adult living with the minor children, but they also live with the father who is married to the executor, and that the petitioner has been very responsive in the last couple of weeks. The Petitions for Approval of Second Interim Account and Report of Executor are continued to October 9, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-01-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To Jun-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-19-303207 Estate of Linda Yee Lin Ma and
PES-19-303208 Estate of Matthew Shui Sang Ma.

Appearnces: Amy Hespenheide appeared by zoom for Melissa Ma (Executor).

Ms. Hespenheide stated that she will submit notification of new counsel within the firm. The Court noted that neither a petition for final distribution nor a petition for approval of report on status of administration has been filed. Ms. Hespenheide asked for a little more time to file her petitions, stated that the only asset is a property in San Francisco which was co-owned by the Decedents, that the wills direct that 10% of the estate pass to each of the charitable organizations, that the remaining 90% pass to a testamentary trust, that Melissa is the Trustee, and that Melissa and her Children are the Beneficiaries. Ms. Hespenheide also stated that there aren't enough liquid funds in the estate to make the charitable gifts, that her Client needs to make a decision as to whether she is going to sell or loan funds to make the charitable distributions, and that the inventory and appraisal was filed in August. In addition, Ms. Hespenheide stated that the need to establish a guardianship of the estate for the Minors is not accurate, that the residue of the estate is to be passed to a testamentary trust and that she does not believe a guardianship of the estate is required for the Minors. Status on close of estate (2) are continued to June 1, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Hespenheide is to file either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration by May 25, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
